facts.text: When Harry Met Sally (1989) was written by Nora Ephron, who drew on her own romantic experiences to craft the screenplay.
The famous deli scene in When Harry Met Sally was filmed at Katz's Delicatessen in New York City, which still has a sign marking the table where it was shot.
Pretty Woman was originally written as a dark drama called '3,000' about a drug-addicted sex worker, before being reworked into a romantic comedy.
Pretty Woman was released on March 23, 1990, and went on to gross over $463 million worldwide.
Hugh Grant has said that the scene in Notting Hill where he climbs over the garden fence was one of the most physically demanding things he's done on film.
The 'meet-cute' is one of the oldest rom-com tropes, describing the charming first encounter between two future love interests.
Friends debuted on NBC on September 22, 1994, and became one of the most beloved ensemble comedies in television history.
Meg Ryan and Tom Hanks starred together in three romantic comedies: Joe Versus the Volcano (1990), Sleepless in Seattle (1993), and You've Got Mail (1998).
Julia Roberts was paid $300,000 for Pretty Woman, but by her next rom-com she was commanding $7 million per film.
The Princess Bride (1987) was a box office disappointment on release but became one of the most quoted films of all time through home video.
Sandra Bullock was 45 years old when she starred in The Proposal (2009), proving rom-com leads don't have an expiration date.
Nora Ephron directed only five films in her career, but three of them — Sleepless in Seattle, You've Got Mail, and Julie & Julia — became cultural touchstones.
In Bridget Jones's Diary, Renée Zellweger gained about 20 pounds and learned a British accent to play the iconic character.
The fake dating trope, where two characters pretend to be a couple and then fall in love for real, has been a rom-com staple since at least the 1950s.
My Big Fat Greek Wedding (2002) was made for just $5 million and grossed over $368 million worldwide, making it one of the most profitable films ever.
The TV show New Girl ran for seven seasons from 2011 to 2018, with Zooey Deschanel playing the quirky and lovable Jess Day.
Richard Curtis wrote and directed Love Actually (2003), which featured an ensemble cast of 10 intertwined love stories set during Christmas.
Crazy Rich Asians was released on August 15, 2018, becoming the first major Hollywood studio film with an all-Asian cast since The Joy Luck Club in 1993.
Matthew McConaughey starred in a string of rom-coms in the early 2000s before his dramatic 'McConaissance' era began.
The enemies-to-lovers trope — where two characters who can't stand each other gradually fall in love — can be traced back to Shakespeare's Much Ado About Nothing.
Annie Hall (1977) won four Academy Awards, including Best Picture, making it one of the few romantic comedies to achieve that honor.
Roman Holiday (1953) starred Audrey Hepburn in her first major role, which won her the Academy Award for Best Actress.
Drew Barrymore and Adam Sandler have co-starred in three rom-coms: The Wedding Singer (1998), 50 First Dates (2004), and Blended (2014).
The Proposal (2009) features Sandra Bullock and Ryan Reynolds, who had such great chemistry that audiences demanded a sequel for years.
Garry Marshall directed both Pretty Woman (1990) and Runaway Bride (1999), reuniting Julia Roberts and Richard Gere.
In How I Met Your Mother, the show's creators planned the ending from the very first season, filming the kids' final reactions early on.
Sleepless in Seattle (1993) was inspired by An Affair to Remember (1957), and the older film plays a key role in the plot.
10 Things I Hate About You (1999) is a modern adaptation of Shakespeare's The Taming of the Shrew, set in a high school.
Kate Hudson became a rom-com icon after her breakout in Almost Famous (2000), followed by How to Lose a Guy in 10 Days (2003).
The airport chase scene, where one character runs through an airport to stop the other from leaving, became such a common trope that modern rom-coms often parody it.
Schitt's Creek swept the Emmys in 2020, winning all seven comedy categories — a first in Emmy history.
Rob Reiner directed both When Harry Met Sally (1989) and The Princess Bride (1987), two of the most beloved romantic films ever made.
In Notting Hill (1999), the blue door of William Thacker's flat became so famous that tourists flocked to the real Notting Hill location to photograph it.
The term 'rom-com' first appeared in widespread use during the 1990s, though the genre dates back to the screwball comedies of the 1930s.
Jennifer Aniston's rom-com career took off after Friends ended in 2004, with films like Along Came Polly, The Break-Up, and Just Go with It.
Love Actually was released on November 14, 2003, and has since become one of the most popular Christmas films of all time.
Nancy Meyers is known for her beautifully designed kitchens in films like Something's Gotta Give, It's Complicated, and The Intern.
Ted Lasso, which premiered on Apple TV+ in 2020, won multiple Emmy Awards and is celebrated for its warmth and optimism.
The makeover montage trope, where a character transforms their appearance, became iconic through films like She's All That (1999) and The Princess Diaries (2001).
Reese Witherspoon produced and starred in several rom-coms, and her production company Hello Sunshine focuses on female-driven stories.
Hitch (2005) starred Will Smith as a dating consultant, marking one of the first major rom-coms with a Black male lead.
The Big Sick (2017) was based on the real-life courtship of Kumail Nanjiani and Emily V. Gordon, who co-wrote the screenplay together.
Moonstruck (1987) won Cher the Academy Award for Best Actress, and the film's depiction of Italian-American family life became iconic.
Four Weddings and a Funeral was released on May 13, 1994, and launched Hugh Grant to international stardom.
The Bollywood film Dilwale Dulhania Le Jayenge (1995) ran continuously in a Mumbai theater for over 20 years, making it one of the longest-running films in cinema history.
Emily in Paris premiered on Netflix in 2020 and, despite polarizing reviews, became one of the platform's most-watched comedy series.
In 13 Going on 30 (2004), Jennifer Garner's character does the 'Thriller' dance at a party, which became one of the film's most memorable scenes.
You've Got Mail (1998) was one of the first films to feature the internet as a central plot device, with AOL Instant Messenger playing a key role.
Sweet Home Alabama (2002) opened at number one at the box office, earning $35 million in its opening weekend.
The K-drama Crash Landing on You (2019-2020) became a global sensation and is credited with helping spread the Korean Wave worldwide.
Never Have I Ever, created by Mindy Kaling and Lang Fisher, ran for four seasons on Netflix and was praised for its representation of Indian-American culture.
27 Dresses (2008) starred Katherine Heigl as a woman who had been a bridesmaid 27 times, wearing a different dress each time.
The line 'I'll have what she's having' from When Harry Met Sally was delivered by Estelle Reiner, the real-life mother of director Rob Reiner.
Richard Curtis also created the Blackadder series and co-founded Comic Relief, showcasing his range beyond rom-coms.
To All the Boys I've Loved Before was released on Netflix on August 17, 2018, and became one of the platform's most-viewed original films.
Crazy Rich Asians was based on Kevin Kwan's best-selling 2013 novel of the same name.
In The Princess Bride, Mandy Patinkin has said that his favorite line — 'Hello. My name is Inigo Montoya.' — is also the line fans quote to him most.
The French film Amélie (2001) became an international hit, grossing over $174 million and earning five Oscar nominations.
Set It Up (2018) is a Netflix original rom-com about two overworked assistants who scheme to set up their demanding bosses.
Jane the Virgin, which ran from 2014 to 2019, was a loving satirical homage to Latin American telenovelas.
Tom Hanks said that filming the final scene atop the Empire State Building in Sleepless in Seattle was freezing cold and took multiple nights.
The Wedding Singer (1998) is set in 1985 and features an extensive soundtrack of 1980s hits that helped make it a nostalgic favorite.
Always Be My Maybe (2019) starred Ali Wong and Randall Park, and featured a memorable celebrity cameo by Keanu Reeves playing a parody of himself.
In Notting Hill, Julia Roberts played the 'most famous actress in the world,' a character loosely inspired by her own real-life fame.
Bridget Jones's Diary was released on April 13, 2001, and was based on Helen Fielding's bestselling novel, itself inspired by Jane Austen's Pride and Prejudice.
The Italian film Cinema Paradiso (1988) features one of cinema's most romantic endings, with a montage of censored kissing scenes.
Sandra Bullock and Keanu Reeves had so much on-screen chemistry in Speed (1994) that fans campaigned for them to do a rom-com together for decades.
The 'manic pixie dream girl' trope was coined by critic Nathan Rabin in 2007, inspired by Kirsten Dunst's character in Elizabethtown.
My Big Fat Greek Wedding was written by and starred Nia Vardalos, who based the story on her own experiences growing up in a Greek family.
How to Lose a Guy in 10 Days (2003) was based on a short humor book of the same name by Michele Alexander and Jeannie Long.
The rom-com genre experienced a decline in the late 2000s and early 2010s, with studios opting for franchise films instead.
Hugh Grant has starred in more than a dozen romantic comedies, earning him the unofficial title of 'King of the Rom-Com.'
Ticket to Paradise (2022) reunited Julia Roberts and George Clooney, and grossed over $321 million worldwide.
The K-drama format typically runs for one season of 16-20 episodes, allowing for a complete romantic story arc without filler.
Anyone But You (2023) starred Sydney Sweeney and Glen Powell and was based on Shakespeare's Much Ado About Nothing.
In Pretty Woman, the famous jewelry box scene where Richard Gere snaps the necklace case on Julia Roberts's fingers was improvised.
Nancy Meyers' films are known for depicting aspirational upper-middle-class lifestyles, and her set designs have influenced interior décor trends.
Schitt's Creek was created by father-and-son duo Eugene and Dan Levy, and premiered on CBC on January 13, 2015.
The screwball comedy It Happened One Night (1934) swept all five major Oscars — Picture, Director, Actor, Actress, and Screenplay.
Reese Witherspoon's Legally Blonde (2001) combined rom-com elements with a feminist message about not underestimating women.
The meet-cute in Sleepless in Seattle doesn't happen until the final moments of the film, making the audience wait the entire movie.
Love Actually's opening montage at Heathrow Airport used hidden cameras to film real reunions between travelers and loved ones.
Four Weddings and a Funeral was made on a modest budget of about $4.4 million and grossed over $245 million worldwide.
The line 'As you wish' from The Princess Bride has become one of the most romantic and frequently quoted lines in film history.
Bollywood produces hundreds of romantic films each year, far outpacing Hollywood in sheer volume of love stories.
Annie Hall was released on April 20, 1977, and is widely considered one of Woody Allen's finest filmmaking achievements.
Friends ended on May 6, 2004, with 52.5 million viewers tuning in — the most-watched entertainment broadcast of the decade.
The 'will they/won't they' dynamic, famously used for Ross and Rachel on Friends, has become a defining rom-com TV trope.
Matthew McConaughey and Kate Hudson co-starred in both How to Lose a Guy in 10 Days (2003) and Fool's Gold (2008).
In When Harry Met Sally, Billy Crystal improvised many of Harry's funniest lines, including several of the New Year's Eve party scenes.
The Proposal (2009) was shot in various Massachusetts locations, though the film is set in Sitka, Alaska.
Crazy Rich Asians director Jon M. Chu turned down a lucrative Netflix deal to ensure the film had a theatrical release.
Dilwale Dulhania Le Jayenge star Shah Rukh Khan is often called the 'King of Bollywood' and has appeared in over 100 films.
10 Things I Hate About You featured Heath Ledger in one of his earliest roles, singing 'Can't Take My Eyes Off You' on the school bleachers.
The concept of 'rom-com season' traditionally referred to the months of February through May, when studios released romantic films.
Nora Ephron began her career as a journalist and essayist before transitioning to screenwriting and directing.
The Big Sick (2017) was produced by Judd Apatow and earned an Academy Award nomination for Best Original Screenplay.
In 13 Going on 30, the 'Thriller' dance scene was not originally in the script; director Gary Winick added it during production.
Clueless (1995), directed by Amy Heckerling, is a loose adaptation of Jane Austen's Emma set in a Beverly Hills high school.
You've Got Mail was a remake of the 1940 film The Shop Around the Corner, starring Jimmy Stewart and Margaret Sullavan.
Ted Lasso was originally created as a character for NBC Sports promotional videos before being developed into a full series.
The rom-com resurgence of the late 2010s was largely fueled by streaming platforms like Netflix, which invested heavily in the genre.
Moonstruck (1987) was released on December 18 and became a holiday season favorite.
Julia Roberts was reportedly not the first choice for Pretty Woman — the role was originally offered to several other actresses, including Molly Ringwald.
In Bridget Jones's Diary, Colin Firth plays Mark Darcy, a character directly based on Mr. Darcy from Pride and Prejudice — which Firth had already played in a 1995 BBC adaptation.
The French rom-com genre, sometimes called 'comédie romantique,' often takes a more philosophical approach to love than its Hollywood counterpart.
Garry Marshall also directed Valentine's Day (2010) and New Year's Eve (2011), both ensemble rom-coms tied to holidays.
Sweet Home Alabama features the line 'So I can kiss you anytime I want,' which has been widely shared as one of the most romantic movie quotes.
How I Met Your Mother premiered on September 19, 2005, and would run for nine seasons before its polarizing finale.
The fake relationship trope appeared in The Proposal, To All the Boys I've Loved Before, and countless other rom-coms.
27 Dresses was filmed in various locations around Rhode Island and New York.
Hugh Grant's bumbling, self-deprecating persona in films like Notting Hill was so effective that it became nearly inseparable from his public image.
Set It Up was one of the first Netflix original rom-coms to receive strong critical praise, with a 92% Rotten Tomatoes score.
In The Princess Bride, Cary Elwes (Westley) actually got knocked unconscious during a sword fighting scene when Christopher Guest accidentally hit him on the head.
The romantic comedy genre traces its roots to ancient Greek playwright Menander, who wrote comedies about young lovers overcoming obstacles.
Emily in Paris features prominent product placements and fashion partnerships, blurring the line between entertainment and advertising.
Drew Barrymore's performance in Never Been Kissed (1999) showed her transition from child star to rom-com leading lady.
The Wedding Planner (2001) was released the same weekend as Jennifer Lopez's album 'J.Lo,' making her the first person to have both a number-one film and album simultaneously.
Notting Hill was released on May 28, 1999, and became the highest-grossing British film of that year.
The line 'I'm just a girl, standing in front of a boy, asking him to love her' from Notting Hill is one of the most quoted rom-com lines ever.
Korean drama Goblin (2016-2017) blended fantasy and romance, becoming one of the highest-rated K-dramas in cable television history.
Love Actually features a storyline where the British Prime Minister dances through 10 Downing Street to 'Jump' by the Pointer Sisters.
Sleepless in Seattle was released on June 25, 1993, and earned over $227 million worldwide.
Jennifer Aniston earned $1 million per episode of Friends during the final two seasons, a record-setting salary at the time.
The Italian romantic comedy Bread and Tulips (2000) won nine David di Donatello awards, Italy's equivalent of the Oscars.
Pretty Woman was originally going to end with Edward leaving Vivian on the street, but test audiences overwhelmingly rejected the dark ending.
New Girl's Nick and Jess first kiss in season 2 was ranked by multiple outlets as one of the best TV kisses of the 2010s.
The Princess Bride was released on September 25, 1987, and initially only performed modestly at the box office.
Meg Ryan was considered Hollywood's 'Queen of Rom-Coms' throughout the 1990s.
In My Big Fat Greek Wedding, the father uses Windex as a cure for everything, a running gag based on Nia Vardalos's real father's habits.
The Proposal was partly inspired by classic screwball comedies where strong-willed women and reluctant men are forced together.
Anyone But You (2023) earned over $220 million worldwide on a $25 million budget, reviving hopes for the theatrical rom-com.
The rom-com 'grand gesture' — a dramatic declaration of love, often in a public setting — has been a genre staple since at least the 1980s.
Four Weddings and a Funeral also starred Andie MacDowell, Kristin Scott Thomas, and Rowan Atkinson as an amusingly inept priest.
Schitt's Creek's David and Patrick wedding in the series finale became one of the most celebrated TV moments of 2020.
Nancy Meyers' The Holiday (2006) popularized the concept of 'home exchange' vacations and became a perennial Christmas favorite.
Tom Hanks has said that Sleepless in Seattle was one of the trickiest films he ever made because he and Meg Ryan had almost no scenes together.
The Bollywood film Kuch Kuch Hota Hai (1998) is considered one of the greatest Indian romantic comedies ever made.
Hitch (2005) was released on February 11, just before Valentine's Day, a strategic move that helped it open at number one.
13 Going on 30 was released on April 23, 2004, and has since become a cult favorite rom-com.
In Clueless, Alicia Silverstone's Cher Horowitz became a fashion icon, with her plaid skirt set and knee-high socks influencing 1990s trends.
Richard Curtis's rom-coms tend to feature ensemble casts and are often set during specific holidays or celebrations.
The Big Sick earned $56 million on a $5 million budget, proving that personal, diverse rom-com stories could succeed commercially.
Matthew McConaughey's rom-com era included The Wedding Planner (2001), How to Lose a Guy in 10 Days (2003), and Failure to Launch (2006).
When Harry Met Sally sparked a cultural debate about whether men and women can truly be 'just friends.'
In Love Actually, the cards scene where Mark (Andrew Lincoln) silently declares his love to Juliet was filmed on a real London street.
The K-drama My Love from the Star (2013-2014) was so popular in China that it reportedly caused a spike in fried chicken and beer sales.
Jane the Virgin star Gina Rodriguez won a Golden Globe for her role, bringing wider attention to Latinx representation in rom-com TV.
The romcom 'love triangle' trope creates tension by introducing a third person who threatens the main couple's happiness.
Legally Blonde was released on July 13, 2001, and Reese Witherspoon's portrayal of Elle Woods became a pop culture phenomenon.
Nora Ephron's essays about her personal life, including her divorce from Carl Bernstein, directly informed her razor-sharp rom-com screenplays.
In the original ending of Pretty Woman, Vivian and her friend use Edward's money to go to Disneyland instead of a fairy tale romance.
The bookshop scene in Notting Hill where Julia Roberts's character browses the travel section was partially improvised.
The 'Hallmark movie' has become its own rom-com sub-genre, with the Hallmark Channel producing over 100 original romantic films per year.
Always Be My Maybe's Keanu Reeves cameo was kept a closely guarded secret during production.
Sandra Bullock's Miss Congeniality (2000) blended rom-com elements with action-comedy, grossing over $212 million.
The rom-com 'bet' trope — where someone makes a wager involving romance — appears in She's All That, How to Lose a Guy, and 10 Things I Hate About You.
To All the Boys I've Loved Before launched Noah Centineo to heartthrob status practically overnight.
Groundhog Day (1993), while primarily a comedy, has a rom-com structure at its core as Bill Murray's character learns to be a better person for love.
New Girl premiered on September 20, 2011, and Zooey Deschanel received a Golden Globe nomination for her role.
The line 'Nobody puts Baby in a corner' from Dirty Dancing (1987) is one of the most famous rom-com movie quotes.
In Crazy Rich Asians, the mahjong scene between Rachel and Eleanor was a pivotal dramatic moment that transformed a game into an emotional confrontation.
Hugh Grant and Julia Roberts had such natural chemistry in Notting Hill that many assumed they were a real-life couple.
The French film The Intouchables (2011), while more of a buddy comedy, features romantic subplots that helped it become France's second highest-grossing film ever.
Garry Marshall got his start directing TV shows like Happy Days and Laverne & Shirley before moving to romantic comedy films.
Bridget Jones's Diary grossed over $281 million worldwide and spawned two sequels.
The rom-com 'mistaken identity' trope dates back to Shakespeare and has been used in films like While You Were Sleeping (1995).
Kate Hudson received a Golden Globe nomination for her first rom-com lead in How to Lose a Guy in 10 Days (2003).
While You Were Sleeping (1995) starred Sandra Bullock as a lonely transit worker who pretends to be the fiancée of a comatose stranger.
Ted Lasso's rom-com sensibility — optimism, emotional growth, and the power of kindness — set it apart from other TV comedies.
In Annie Hall, Woody Allen broke the fourth wall by having characters speak directly to the camera, a technique that influenced many later rom-coms.
The Proposal earned over $317 million worldwide on a $40 million budget.
K-drama Boys Over Flowers (2009) was one of the first Korean dramas to gain an international following and boosted the careers of its entire cast.
10 Things I Hate About You was released on March 31, 1999.
My Big Fat Greek Wedding held the record for the highest-grossing romantic comedy in North America until the 2010s.
The Rom-Com renaissance of the 2020s saw studios return to the genre after years of neglect, buoyed by streaming platform investments.
In Moonstruck, Cher's character falls for her fiancé's brother, played by Nicolas Cage, in a plot that celebrates messy, passionate love.
Rob Reiner based When Harry Met Sally partly on his own experiences reentering the dating world after his divorce.
The rom-com 'rain kiss' scene — where two characters share a passionate kiss in the rain — was popularized by films like Breakfast at Tiffany's and The Notebook.
Emily in Paris became Netflix's most-watched comedy series in many countries when it debuted in 2020.
Drew Barrymore produced and starred in 50 First Dates (2004), which was filmed in Hawaii and grossed over $196 million.
When Harry Met Sally was released on July 21, 1989, and became a defining film of the genre.
The 'slow clap' moment, often occurring after a public declaration of love, became a rom-com cliché by the early 2000s.
Sweet Home Alabama (2002) was the number-one film at the box office for three consecutive weeks.
Never Have I Ever premiered on Netflix on April 27, 2020.
In You've Got Mail, Tom Hanks' character quotes The Godfather, leading to a now-famous dialogue about how men view that film.
Crazy Rich Asians earned over $238 million worldwide and received a sequel greenlight almost immediately.
The Princess Bride was adapted by William Goldman from his own 1973 novel of the same name.
Jennifer Aniston's rom-com Just Go with It (2011) grossed over $214 million worldwide despite middling reviews.
The Bollywood film Jab We Met (2007) revitalized Kareena Kapoor's career and is considered a modern Bollywood rom-com classic.
In 27 Dresses, Katherine Heigl's character keeps all 27 bridesmaid dresses in her closet — a visual gag that opens the film.
Nora Ephron's last feature film was Julie & Julia (2009), which earned Meryl Streep an Oscar nomination.
The rom-com 'makeover reveal' moment — where a transformed character descends a staircase — is directly borrowed from the Cinderella fairy tale.
How I Met Your Mother's finale in 2014 divided fans, with many feeling the ending undermined the show's central romantic premise.
Four Weddings and a Funeral received two Academy Award nominations: Best Picture and Best Original Screenplay.
Clueless director Amy Heckerling spent months researching Beverly Hills teen culture to make the dialogue feel authentic.
The Italian film La Dolce Vita (1960) influenced rom-com aesthetics for decades with its glamorous depiction of Roman nightlife.
In Sleepless in Seattle, the character of Jonah was originally conceived as much less prominent, but Ross Malinger's audition expanded the role.
Matthew McConaughey's Failure to Launch (2006) featured a subplot where his character literally cannot leave his parents' house.
Richard Curtis wrote the screenplay for Bridget Jones's Diary alongside Helen Fielding and Andrew Davies.
The concept of 'shipping' — wanting two characters to get together — has its roots in rom-com fandom.
Love Actually featured an iconic opening sequence filmed at Heathrow Airport's arrivals gate showcasing real people reuniting.
Sandra Bullock and Ryan Reynolds improvised several scenes in The Proposal, including the naked collision in the hallway.
The Wedding Singer featured a scene where Adam Sandler sings on an airplane, which was shot on a real 747 set.
Notting Hill's soundtrack, featuring songs like 'She' by Elvis Costello, became one of the best-selling rom-com soundtracks.
Mindy Kaling's The Mindy Project (2012-2017) blended rom-com tropes with sharp commentary on modern dating.
The Princess Bride's line 'Inconceivable!' became one of the most quoted comedy catchphrases of the 1980s.
Ticket to Paradise was filmed in Queensland, Australia, though it's set in Bali, Indonesia.
In Hitch, Will Smith's dance moves during an allergic reaction scene were largely improvised.
Roman Holiday featured Audrey Hepburn and Gregory Peck exploring Rome on a Vespa, creating an iconic image that still defines the city's romantic appeal.
The 'second lead syndrome' in K-dramas refers to audiences falling for the character who doesn't end up with the protagonist.
Schitt's Creek premiered with modest ratings but built its audience steadily to become one of the most acclaimed comedies of its era.
Julia Roberts earned an Oscar nomination for Pretty Woman, cementing her status as Hollywood's leading lady.
The 'rom-com montage' — a sequence set to pop music showing the couple's growing relationship — became codified in the 1980s.
Garry Marshall directed his sister Penny Marshall's first husband, Rob Reiner, early in his career on TV shows.
The Apartment (1960), directed by Billy Wilder, won the Academy Award for Best Picture and is considered one of the greatest romantic comedies ever made.
Anyone But You was filmed in Sydney, Australia, and showcased several scenic Australian locations.
In To All the Boys I've Loved Before, the contract between Lara Jean and Peter Kavinsky became one of the memorable props in modern rom-com history.
Drew Barrymore and Hugh Grant starred together in Music and Lyrics (2007), a rom-com about songwriting.
The Big Sick was released on July 14, 2017, and earned widespread critical acclaim.
Meg Ryan's signature tousled blonde hairstyle in You've Got Mail became widely imitated in the late 1990s.
The Kissing Booth (2018) became one of Netflix's most-watched films and launched a trilogy.
In Annie Hall, the famous lobster scene was partly improvised by Woody Allen and Diane Keaton.
Reese Witherspoon and Vince Vaughn co-starred in Four Christmases (2008), a holiday rom-com about a couple visiting all four of their divorced parents.
The Bollywood film Queen (2014) subverted rom-com expectations by having the heroine find happiness independently after being left at the altar.
Hugh Grant's arrest in 1995 actually boosted the box office of his rom-com Nine Months, which opened shortly after.
Nancy Meyers' first writing credit was on the 1980 film Private Benjamin, which earned her an Oscar nomination.
The 'best friend who gives advice' is one of the most reliable rom-com supporting character archetypes.
In When Harry Met Sally, Sally's ordering habits at restaurants became a relatable running joke that many viewers identified with.
About Time (2013), written and directed by Richard Curtis, combined rom-com elements with a time-travel premise.
My Big Fat Greek Wedding 2 was released 14 years after the original, in 2016.
The rom-com 'wedding crasher' scenario became its own sub-genre, with Owen Wilson and Vince Vaughn leading the charge in Wedding Crashers (2005).
Jennifer Aniston's The Morning Show showcased her dramatic range, but audiences continued to associate her most with rom-com and comedy roles.
Crazy Rich Asians' wedding scene was set to 'Can't Help Falling in Love' sung in Mandarin by Kina Grannis, creating a viral moment.
10 Things I Hate About You starred Julia Stiles, who later went on to appear in more dramatic roles in the Bourne franchise.
The Princess Bride initially struggled at the box office, earning only $30.9 million domestically, but became one of the most beloved cult films of all time.
In Notting Hill, the changing seasons montage as Hugh Grant walks through the market was filmed over the course of one year.
The 'love letter' trope has appeared in rom-coms from Cyrano de Bergerac adaptations to To All the Boys I've Loved Before.
Kate Hudson and Matthew McConaughey's on-screen chemistry in How to Lose a Guy in 10 Days was aided by the fact that they are friends in real life.
Set It Up's positive reception convinced Netflix to invest more heavily in original romantic comedies.
Moonstruck was released on December 18, 1987, and went on to gross $80 million domestically.
Bridget Jones's Baby (2016) was the third film in the Bridget Jones franchise and introduced a love triangle with Patrick Dempsey.
The K-drama Descendants of the Sun (2016) was a pre-produced series, meaning it was fully filmed before airing — unusual for Korean television.
Rob Reiner based Billy Crystal's character in When Harry Met Sally partly on himself and Meg Ryan's character partly on Nora Ephron.
The line 'You complete me' from Jerry Maguire (1996) became one of the most parodied romantic declarations in pop culture.
Jane the Virgin used on-screen pop-up text to translate Spanish dialogues and provide humorous commentary throughout the series.
Nancy Meyers directed Something's Gotta Give (2003), where Jack Nicholson and Diane Keaton proved that rom-coms could work with older leads.
In Pretty Woman, Julia Roberts's iconic red dress was designed by Marilyn Vance and became one of the most recognized costumes in film history.
The first Bollywood film to be entirely shot in the United States was reportedly Kal Ho Naa Ho (2003), a romantic comedy-drama set in New York.
Failure to Launch (2006) starred Matthew McConaughey and Sarah Jessica Parker and was Sarah Jessica Parker's first major role after Sex and the City ended.
Sleepless in Seattle used the Empire State Building as a romantic meeting point, echoing An Affair to Remember's use of the same landmark.
The rom-com 'fish out of water' trope places a character in an unfamiliar setting, as seen in Crocodile Dundee, Coming to America, and Emily in Paris.
You've Got Mail was released on December 18, 1998, and earned over $250 million worldwide.
Hugh Grant acknowledged he nearly turned down the role in Four Weddings and a Funeral, worried about being typecast.
The rom-com soundtrack became big business in the 1990s, with albums from Pretty Woman, Sleepless in Seattle, and Notting Hill all going multi-platinum.
In How to Lose a Guy in 10 Days, the 'love fern' became a running joke that fans of the film still reference.
Ted Lasso won the Primetime Emmy Award for Outstanding Comedy Series in both 2021 and 2022.
Sandra Bullock won the Razzie for Worst Actress on the same weekend she won the Oscar for Best Actress in 2010, appearing at both ceremonies.
The rom-com 'childhood friends to lovers' trope appeared in 13 Going on 30, where Jenna and Matt had been best friends as children.
Dirty Dancing (1987) was made for $6 million and grossed $218 million worldwide, becoming one of the most successful independent films of the 1980s.
Love Actually has been the subject of annual debate about whether it's genuinely romantic or deeply problematic.
Richard Curtis announced his retirement from directing after About Time (2013), though he has continued to write.
In Crazy Rich Asians, the closing credits mahjong game symbolized strategic social maneuvering, not just a card game.
The Italian romantic comedy Perfetti Sconosciuti (Perfect Strangers, 2016) has been remade in over 20 countries, setting a record.
Jennifer Aniston and Adam Sandler have co-starred in Just Go with It (2011) and Murder Mystery (2019), becoming a popular on-screen pair.
The 'opposites attract' trope is foundational to rom-coms, from The Odd Couple to Every romantic comedy where a free spirit meets a Type-A personality.
Sweet Home Alabama was filmed in Georgia and California, not Alabama.
To All the Boys I've Loved Before spawned two sequels: P.S. I Still Love You (2020) and Always and Forever (2021).
Reese Witherspoon's Walk the Line (2005) was more of a biopic than a rom-com, but it showcased the romantic chemistry between her and Joaquin Phoenix.
The 'Mamma Mia!' film franchise (2008, 2018) blended musical theater with rom-com elements, grossing over $1 billion combined.
Hitch was one of the top-grossing films of 2005, earning over $368 million worldwide.
In Schitt's Creek, the Rose family's relationships evolved from dysfunctional to deeply loving over the course of the series.
The rom-com 'transformation' theme — where love makes someone a better person — is central to classics like Groundhog Day and As Good as It Gets.
Moonstruck was written by John Patrick Shanley, who won the Academy Award for Best Original Screenplay for the film.
The Korean drama format's emphasis on slow-burn romance and emotional depth has influenced Western rom-com television writing.
27 Dresses was released on January 18, 2008.
Meg Ryan turned down the lead role in Sleepless in Seattle twice before finally accepting.
The rom-com 'big misunderstanding' trope, where a solvable problem creates conflict, has been criticized by modern audiences as contrived.
My Big Fat Greek Wedding was initially released in a limited run before word of mouth turned it into a massive hit.
Nancy Meyers' The Intern (2015) starred Robert De Niro and Anne Hathaway and addressed age differences in the modern workplace through a rom-com-adjacent lens.
In Four Weddings and a Funeral, Hugh Grant's character attends four weddings, at each one encountering the American woman he's falling for.
Pretty Woman's 'shopping on Rodeo Drive' scene has inspired tourist visits to Beverly Hills for decades.
The 'prom scene' resolution, where everything comes together at a school dance, is a defining feature of teen rom-coms like She's All That and 10 Things I Hate About You.
Anyone But You's success at the box office in late 2023 was partly attributed to the real-life chemistry and social media presence of its leads.
Nora Ephron once said the key to a great rom-com is that each half of the couple must be someone the audience would want to be with.
In Love Actually, the storyline involving Colin traveling to America was the most criticized plot, yet many fans consider it the funniest.
Tom Hanks received a $5 million salary for Sleepless in Seattle, which was considered moderate for a top star at the time.
The Bollywood film Band Baaja Baaraat (2010) launched Ranveer Singh's career and is considered a modern romantic comedy classic in India.
Clueless was released on July 19, 1995, and launched Alicia Silverstone's career.
The 'six degrees of separation' concept has been explored in several rom-coms, most notably in Valentine's Day (2010).
In The Princess Bride, the word 'inconceivable' is said by Vizzini exactly six times throughout the film.
Sex and the City (1998-2004) blended rom-com elements with frank discussions of female sexuality and friendship.
Julia Roberts was paid $20 million for My Best Friend's Wedding (1997), making her one of the highest-paid actresses in Hollywood.
The 'wrong person at the altar' trope — where a character realizes they're marrying the wrong person — has been used in films from The Graduate to Runaway Bride.
Sandra Bullock's Two Weeks Notice (2002) paired her with Hugh Grant and earned over $199 million worldwide.
Crazy Rich Asians was released during a period when Asian representation in Hollywood was heavily debated, making it culturally significant beyond its entertainment value.
In The Big Sick, Kumail Nanjiani performs stand-up comedy — a reflection of his real career — while navigating his relationship with Emily.
The 'holiday rom-com' sub-genre generates massive viewership every November and December, with networks like Hallmark and Lifetime producing dozens each year.
Kate Hudson's rom-com Bride Wars (2009) with Anne Hathaway explored female friendship and competition.
Matthew McConaughey's iconic 'alright, alright, alright' catchphrase originated from Dazed and Confused (1993), not a rom-com.
The Notebook (2004), while more of a romantic drama, popularized many rom-com tropes including the rain kiss and the love letter.
Garry Marshall's Pretty Woman was the third highest-grossing film of 1990, behind Home Alone and Ghost.
In 10 Things I Hate About You, the poem Kat reads at the end was written by the screenwriters, not adapted from Shakespeare.
The K-drama What's Wrong with Secretary Kim (2018) was based on a popular web novel and became one of the most-watched rom-com K-dramas globally.
Never Have I Ever's Devi Vishwakumar was one of the first Indian-American protagonists in a teen rom-com TV series.
Richard Curtis's filmmaking style is characterized by multiple intercut storylines, British humor, and sentimental endings.
The 'workplace romance' trope has been central to rom-coms from His Girl Friday (1940) to Set It Up (2018).
Bridget Jones's Diary was released on April 13, 2001, in the United States.
In Sleepless in Seattle, the letters to the radio show were inspired by a real trend of people calling into talk shows for dating advice in the early 1990s.
Hugh Grant's dance to 'Jump' in Love Actually was entirely his own idea and was unrehearsed.
The rom-com 'second chance romance' trope — where former lovers reconnect — drives films like Mamma Mia!, Ticket to Paradise, and It's Complicated.
My Big Fat Greek Wedding was released on April 19, 2002.
Jennifer Aniston was offered the role of Rachel Green on Friends after the producers saw her audition for another show entirely.
The fire escape scene in Pretty Woman, where Edward climbs up to Vivian with flowers, was inspired by fairy tale imagery.
Rob Reiner launched Castle Rock Entertainment, which produced When Harry Met Sally and many other landmark films.
The Proposal (2009) was released on June 19 and became a summer comedy hit.
The French romantic comedy Heartbreaker (L'Arnacœur, 2010) featured a memorable scene recreating the Dirty Dancing lift.
In Annie Hall, the lobsters that Alvy and Annie try to cook become a metaphor for their chaotic but endearing relationship.
Reese Witherspoon's production company Hello Sunshine helped bring Where the Crawdads Sing and other romantic stories to the screen.
The 'group of friends' ensemble rom-com, as seen in Friends, How I Met Your Mother, and New Girl, allows for multiple romantic storylines simultaneously.
Ticket to Paradise was George Clooney's return to the rom-com genre after O Brother, Where Art Thou? and One Fine Day.
The K-drama Itaewon Class (2020) blended entrepreneurial ambition with romance and became a worldwide Netflix hit.
In You've Got Mail, the Fox Books store that puts Kathleen Kelly's small shop out of business was inspired by the real-life rise of Barnes & Noble and Borders.
Crazy Rich Asians' production designer Nelson Coates created lavish sets that became aspirational images shared widely on social media.
Drew Barrymore and Jimmy Fallon starred in Fever Pitch (2005), a rom-com set against the backdrop of the Boston Red Sox's historic 2004 season.
The 'unrequited love' theme in rom-coms generates sympathy and suspense, as audiences root for the character to finally be noticed.
Hugh Grant's role in Paddington 2 (2017) showed he could reinvent himself beyond rom-coms, playing a vain and villainous actor.
Notting Hill was written by Richard Curtis and was his follow-up to the massive success of Four Weddings and a Funeral.
The rom-com 'dance scene' — from Dirty Dancing to 13 Going on 30 — provides a physical expression of emotional connection.
In Love Actually, Emma Thompson's crying scene after discovering her husband's affair was achieved in a single take.
How to Lose a Guy in 10 Days was released on February 7, 2003, strategically placed before Valentine's Day.
The first film generally considered a romantic comedy is It Happened One Night (1934), directed by Frank Capra.
Nancy Meyers' Baby Boom (1987) starred Diane Keaton as a businesswoman whose life changes when she inherits a baby.
The 'small town charm' trope, where a city person discovers love in a rural setting, is a staple of Hallmark rom-coms and films like Sweet Home Alabama.
In 13 Going on 30, Mark Ruffalo's understated performance as Matt was widely praised as the ideal rom-com love interest.
Friends' Central Perk coffee shop set has been recreated as pop-up experiences in cities around the world.
The Bollywood film Yeh Jawaani Hai Deewani (2013) grossed over $50 million worldwide and is one of the highest-grossing Bollywood rom-coms of all time.
In Bridget Jones's Diary, the fight scene between Colin Firth and Hugh Grant on the street was choreographed to look intentionally clumsy and realistic.
Sandra Bullock's Speed 2: Cruise Control (1997) was a rare rom-com sequel miss, earning poor reviews despite the original's success.
The 'love at first sight' trope, while often critiqued as unrealistic, remains a beloved rom-com convention.
Schitt's Creek won a total of nine Primetime Emmy Awards during its run.
The Princess Bride director Rob Reiner originally wanted Arnold Schwarzenegger to play Fezzik before casting André the Giant.
In Set It Up, Glen Powell's performance caught industry attention and helped launch his later career as a leading man.
The Wedding Singer was released on February 13, 1998, the day before Valentine's Day.
The rom-com 'quirky best friend' archetype provides comic relief and often serves as the protagonist's sounding board.
In Moonstruck, Olympia Dukakis won the Academy Award for Best Supporting Actress for playing Cher's mother.
Meg Ryan and Billy Crystal remained close friends for decades after filming When Harry Met Sally.
The K-drama format's use of cliffhangers at the end of each episode keeps viewers binge-watching romantic storylines.
Tom Hanks' career spans both comedic and dramatic roles, but his rom-com work with Meg Ryan defined a generation's idea of cinematic romance.
Legally Blonde 2: Red, White & Blonde (2003) took Elle Woods to Washington, D.C., but was less well-received than the original.
The 'road trip romance' trope, where a couple falls in love while traveling, has been used from It Happened One Night to The Proposal.
Kate Winslet's performance in The Holiday (2006) earned her another wave of fans who primarily knew her from dramatic roles.
Jane the Virgin premiered on October 13, 2014, on The CW.
In Pretty Woman, the opera scene where Vivian is moved to tears was Julia Roberts genuinely reacting to the music during filming.
The 'enemies to lovers' trope's enduring appeal is often attributed to the tension and chemistry that conflict creates between characters.
Crazy Rich Asians' success led to the development of a sequel based on Kevin Kwan's second novel, China Rich Girlfriend.
Richard Curtis has said that love is the most important thing in life, and that all his films are fundamentally about celebrating it.
The rom-com 'last-minute confession' scene typically takes place at a wedding, airport, or other significant location.
In When Harry Met Sally, Meg Ryan and Billy Crystal reportedly didn't get along well initially, which actually helped their on-screen dynamic of bickering friends.
Matthew McConaughey's 'McConaissance' began around 2011 with The Lincoln Lawyer, taking him from rom-com star to Oscar-winning dramatic actor.
Love Actually was released as both a romantic comedy film and, for many, an annual holiday viewing tradition.
My Big Fat Greek Wedding inspired a short-lived CBS TV series called My Big Fat Greek Life (2003), which lasted only seven episodes.
The rom-com 'bet' in She's All That (1999) involves the popular guy wagering he can turn any girl into prom queen.
Julia Roberts' famous laugh — big, unrestrained, and genuine — became one of her most marketable traits in rom-coms.
The Bollywood industry's 'item number' — a flashy musical sequence — often serves as the romantic set piece in Indian rom-coms.
In Notting Hill, the scene where Hugh Grant pretends to be a journalist from Horse & Hound magazine was not originally in the script.
Always Be My Maybe was released on Netflix on May 31, 2019.
The rom-com 'parent trap' scenario — where children scheme to get their parents together — inspired the film of the same name in both 1961 and 1998.
Sandra Bullock is one of the few actresses to have starred in both successful rom-coms and action films, from The Proposal to Gravity.
In Annie Hall, the split-screen therapy scene was revolutionary in showing how differently the two characters perceived their relationship.
Meg Ryan directed her first rom-com, Ithaca (2015), shifting from in front of the camera to behind it.
The rom-com 'love triangle' in My Best Friend's Wedding (1997) was notable because Julia Roberts's character doesn't get the guy in the end.
While You Were Sleeping was released on April 21, 1995, and became one of Sandra Bullock's most beloved early films.
The French rom-com Amélie inspired a wave of whimsical, visually stylized romantic films across Europe.
In Bridget Jones's Diary, the character's calorie-counting and diary entries resonated with audiences who saw themselves in her imperfect daily struggles.
The Ugly Truth (2009) starred Katherine Heigl and Gerard Butler in a rom-com about a TV producer forced to work with a chauvinistic commentator.
Nora Ephron's Heartburn (1986) was a fictionalized account of her marriage to Carl Bernstein, starring Meryl Streep and Jack Nicholson.
The rom-com trope of 'the one that got away' — reconnecting with a former flame — drives films like High Fidelity and Long Shot.
Long Shot (2019) starred Charlize Theron and Seth Rogen in an unlikely romantic pairing that was praised for its chemistry and humor.
The K-drama Something in the Rain (2018) tackled workplace romance and the social stigma of an older woman dating a younger man.
50 First Dates (2004) had a unique rom-com premise: Drew Barrymore's character has short-term memory loss and forgets Adam Sandler's character every day.
The rom-com 'holiday' sub-genre extends to every major holiday, from Valentine's Day films to Halloween-themed romances on streaming platforms.
Trainwreck (2015, written by Amy Schumer) flipped the rom-com script by making the female lead the commitment-phobic character.
In Four Weddings and a Funeral, the funeral poem 'Stop all the clocks' by W.H. Auden experienced a massive surge in popularity after the film.
The Proposal's scene where Sandra Bullock and Ryan Reynolds dance to 'It Takes Two' around a bonfire was filmed multiple times due to laughter.
Sleepless in Seattle's Jonah writing to a radio show to find his dad a wife was inspired by a real-life call to a talk radio program.
The rom-com 'bookstore meet-cute' trope has been used in Notting Hill, You've Got Mail, and countless Hallmark movies.
Runaway Bride (1999) was the second pairing of Julia Roberts and Richard Gere, and grossed over $309 million worldwide.
The K-drama Full House (2004) was one of the earliest Korean dramas to gain a significant pan-Asian following.
Romantic comedies often use seasonal changes as visual metaphors for the progression of a relationship.
